display.purpose: Page and hero titles. Fixed rem (no fluid clamp) since users view at consistent DPI.

headline.purpose: Section and card titles (CardTitle, text-2xl tracking-tight).

body.purpose: Prose and lesson content. Cap measure at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Badges, chips, short metadata. Uppercase only for <=4-word labels.

mono.purpose: JetBrains Mono — Kotlin code blocks, Monaco editor, dense tables only.

shadows.name: brand-sm
shadows.purpose: Resting card shadow; barely-there. Navy-tinted, not neutral black.

shadows.name: brand-md
shadows.purpose: Hover/active lift on interactive cards and popovers.

shadows.name: brand-lg
shadows.purpose: Modals and the dashboard hero only.

motion.name: interactive
motion.purpose: Default state transitions: hover lift, button press scale(0.98).

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: Active under prefers-reduced-motion: reduce (wired globally in index.css).

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.purpose: Side nav collapses to a drawer below this.

breakpoints.name: xl
breakpoints.purpose: Audited at 375 / 768 / 1440 in the Tier 3 mobile pass.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single decisive action per view — the next step on the path.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: #ffffff; background: #2563eb; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out, transform 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #2160dd; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #ffffff, 0 0 0 4px #2563eb; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: The quiet default for every action that is not the next step.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: #0f172a; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease-out, transform 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #f1f5f9; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #ffffff, 0 0 0 4px #2563eb; } .ds-btn-outline:active { transform: scale(0.98); }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat at rest; lifts on hover when interactive. Navy-tinted shadow, hairline border, no colored side-stripe.
components.html:
Kotlin: Funciones

Aprende a declarar y usar funciones en Kotlin.
components.css: .ds-card { padding: 24px; background: #ffffff; color: #0f172a; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(23,37,84,0.08); transition: transform 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px -2px rgba(23,37,84,0.12), 0 2px 4px -1px rgba(23,37,84,0.06); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 6px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.01em; line-height: 1.2; } .ds-card-desc { margin: 0; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; color: #64748b; line-height: 1.6; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: 44px touch target, hairline border, focus ring + border shift to blue.
components.html: <input class='ds-input' type='text' placeholder='Escribe tu pregunta al tutor...' />
components.css: .ds-input { display: block; width: 100%; height: 44px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; color: #0f172a; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 10px; transition: border-color 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #64748b; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #2563eb; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #ffffff, 0 0 0 4px #2563eb; } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; background: #f1f5f9; }

components.name: Level Badge
components.description: Fully-pill chip for student level and AI-generated tags. Blue by default; gold only when it marks something earned.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 10px; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #ffffff; background: #2563eb; border-radius: 9999px; }

components.name: Module / Step Card
components.description: Signature component: the route made visible. State ring (gray locked / blue in-progress / green done), progress bar, gold check when earned. Must reflect real progress.
components.html:
M2 · Kotlin esencial

60%
3 de 5 temas completados
components.css: .ds-module { padding: 24px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e2e8f0; border-radius: 10px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(23,37,84,0.08); transition: transform 150ms ease-out, box-shadow 150ms ease-out; } .ds-module:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px -2px rgba(23,37,84,0.12), 0 2px 4px -1px rgba(23,37,84,0.06); } .ds-module-top { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; } .ds-module-state { width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 9999px; flex: none; } .ds-module-state--progress { background: #2563eb; } .ds-module-state--locked { background: #9ca3af; } .ds-module-state--done { background: #16a34a; } .ds-module-name { margin: 0; flex: 1; font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; color: #0f172a; } .ds-module-pct { font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; color: #2563eb; } .ds-module-track { margin: 14px 0 8px; height: 8px; background: #e2e8f0; border-radius: 9999px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-module-fill { display: block; height: 100%; background: #2563eb; border-radius: 9999px; } .ds-module-meta { font-family: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; color: #64748b; }

narrative.overview: The interface is a guided climb. A student logs in somewhere on a route — modules are stages, topics are steps, and the system always shows the next foothold rather than the whole mountain. Deep navy is the rock of the route; institutional blue is the path and the current step; heritage gold marks only what the student has earned. The surface underfoot is a calm near-white (or deep navy-black in dark mode) that never competes with content. This is a product surface: design serves the task of learning, with warm institutional confidence — a real institute, thesis-grade, but human and encouraging, never cold and never childish. Progress is a journey with momentum, not a scoreboard of points.
keyCharacteristics: Register: product — the tool disappears into the act of learning.
Identity: institutional navy + earned heritage gold + Andean red accent, on a clean white / deep-navy surface.
One typeface (Plus Jakarta Sans) carries everything; JetBrains Mono for code and data only.
Flat at rest; a navy-tinted lift appears only on interaction.
Progress reads as a route with milestones — blue = current, green = done, gold = earned.
WCAG 2.1 AA, full light + dark parity, mobile-first down to 375px.

rules.name: The Earned-Gold Rule
rules.body: Heritage gold marks only what the student has earned — achievements, completed milestones, the accent bar. Never decoration or a generic highlight. Its scarcity is the reward.

rules.name: The Traffic-Light-Free Rule
rules.body: Module/topic state is gray (locked) -> blue (in progress) -> green (completed). Red is never 'not done'; it is destructive + Peru accent only. State also carries an icon + label, never color alone.

rules.name: The One-Loud-Color Rule
rules.body: Institutional blue is the only saturated color allowed to carry weight on a working screen. Gold and red appear in slivers. If two colors fight for the eye, one is wrong.

rules.name: The One-Voice Rule
rules.body: Plus Jakarta Sans carries headings, buttons, labels, body, and data. No second display typeface; contrast comes from weight and size. JetBrains Mono is the only exception, for code/data.

rules.name: The No-Shout Rule
rules.body: No all-caps body copy. Uppercase is for short labels and badges only. The in-app display ceiling is 2.25rem; the page guides, it does not shout.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat (hairline + brand-sm) at rest. The brand-md lift appears only on hover/focus as feedback. A page of pre-elevated cards is a smell.

dos: Do keep exactly one primary (institutional-blue) action per view — the next step on the path. Everything else is outline or ghost.
Do reserve heritage gold (#f59e0b) for earned achievements and milestone markers only.
Do convey module/topic state with gray -> blue -> green plus an icon and label, never color alone.
Do keep cards flat at rest (brand-sm) and let them lift (brand-md) on hover via .interactive-card.
Do hold body text at >=4.5:1 — ink (#0f172a) or muted-ink (#64748b) on white; the same bar for placeholders.
Do keep one shape vocabulary: buttons rounded-md (8px), cards & inputs rounded-lg (10px), chips & badges rounded-full.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion and keep transitions 150-250ms; motion conveys state, not decoration.
Do make every AI surface degrade gracefully — a fallback (DB quiz bank, coding catalog) must look intentional, never broken.
donts: Don't build a cluttered Moodle-style LMS: no everything-everywhere navigation, no content buried under nested tabs. One primary job per screen.
Don't ship generic template-SaaS slop: no hero-metric template, no endless identical icon-heading-text card grids, no gradient text.
Don't tip 'encouraging' into childish gamification: no cartoon mascots, neon badges, or confetti spam. These are adult technical students.
Don't use a border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe. Use a full border, a background tint, or a leading icon.
Don't put a tiny uppercase tracked eyebrow above every section, or 01/02/03 numbered markers as default scaffolding.
Don't introduce a second display font, new palette hues, or glassmorphism-by-default. Contrast comes from weight and the existing palette.
Don't use light gray for body text 'for elegance' — it fails AA.
Don't use red to mean 'incomplete' — red is destructive + Peru accent only.
